texts.facebook:
Quiz time 🧠 Why doesn't an ML project end after the model is deployed?

A) Documentation must be written
B) Data changes over time — model degrades and needs monitoring/retraining
C) Other teams handle the deployment
D) It never ends — the model works forever

Answer in the comments — I'll post the explanation tomorrow. This is 1 of 120 questions inside ML Career Simulator, where you grow from Junior to Middle ML Engineer through 20 real cases at Datacore. https://ml-simulator-app-production.up.railway.app/en.html?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=organic&utm_content=2026-07-24-quiz-b16fb9
texts.linkedin:
Here's a question that trips up a lot of new ML engineers:

Why doesn't an ML project end after the model is deployed?

A) Because documentation must be written
B) Data changes over time and the model degrades — it needs monitoring and retraining
C) Because other teams handle the deployment
D) It does end — the model works forever

Drop your answer in the comments. I'll share the explanation tomorrow.

This is one of 120 real-world questions built into ML Career Simulator — a course where you're hired as a Junior ML Engineer at fictional company Datacore and grow to Middle through 20 business cases.

https://ml-simulator-app-production.up.railway.app/en.html?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=organic&utm_content=2026-07-24-quiz-b16fb9

#MachineLearning #MLEngineer #DataScience #CareerGrowth #AIJobs
texts.tiktok: Why doesn't an ML project end after deployment? 🤔 Comment your answer! #ml #machinelearning #techcareer Link in bio

card.headline: Why doesn't an ML project end at deployment?
lines: B) Data changes; model needs monitoring
D) It never ends — model works forever
